svg.text: NTSC ARTIFACT COLORS — HOW PIXEL PATTERNS BECOME COLORS
CGA Mode 6 (640×200 Monochrome) — Each group of 4 pixels = 1 color on NTSC composite
CGA's pixel clock in mode 6 is 14.318 MHz —
exactly 4× the NTSC color burst frequency.
Every 4 pixels align with one color cycle.
The on/off pattern within those 4 pixels
determines the color a TV displays.
The V6355D's hidden mode reads each group
of 4 bits as a palette index (0–15).
CGA mode 6 stores 8 pixels per byte —
that's two groups of 4 bits per byte.
We program each palette entry to match
the NTSC artifact color for that pattern.
Same data, same colors — no CPU needed
ALL 16 NIBBLE PATTERNS — MODE 6 ARTIFACT COLORS
Curated by Retro Erik — Mode 6 palette, hand-tuned for CGA composite games on the Olivetti PC1
Curated by Retro Erik — Mode 6 (640×200) — Olivetti Prodest PC1 / Yamaha V6355D — NTSC-16COLOR-TSR
